audit_items.proof_trace: All MethodClosure_Bind*::Run() methods call (get_pointer(m_object)->*m_method)() with no null guard, and get_pointer() (closure_helper.h:14, shared_ptr.hpp:362) performs no null check. However, every identified call site passes non-null pointers: rpc_request.cc uses shared_from_this() storing an owning shared_ptr in the closure (preventing dangling), while all function-closure call sites use address-of static functions (never null). No network-sourced attacker data influences which pointer is stored in a closure — all construction is internal to the server framework, making external null-injection impossible.
